metadata.description: Hybrid Z.AI Pro (reasoning) + Groq (speed) multi-agent orchestration
metadata.strategy: Z.AI GLM-4.7 for orchestration, GLM-4.5 for reasoning tasks, GLM-4.6V for QA/vision, Groq for implementation
metadata.expectedPerformance: 4-5x faster than Lite plan, 3-4x faster implementations, no token quota limits
metadata.keyImprovement: 5x token allocation vs Lite, 40-60% faster inference, vision analysis capabilities

agents.name: orchestrator-primary
agents.description: Master orchestrator - Z.AI GLM-4.7 for task decomposition, state management, agent coordination. CRITICAL bottleneck - use only GLM-4.7.
agents.notes: Now has 5x concurrency (was 2 on Lite). No more orchestration bottlenecks!

agents.name: architect
agents.description: System architect - Z.AI GLM-4.5 for database schemas, API structures, deployment architecture. Downgraded from 4.7 to preserve tokens.
agents.notes: Downgraded to GLM-4.5 (10x concurrency). Saves 4.7 slots for orchestrator.

agents.name: backend-engineer
agents.description: Backend implementation - Groq Llama 3.3 for fast server logic, APIs, database queries.
agents.notes: Groq is free and fast - maximize usage here.

agents.name: frontend-engineer
agents.description: Frontend implementation - Groq Llama 3.3 for fast React/Vue/Next.js component generation.
agents.notes: Groq excels at code generation - this is where it shines.

agents.name: qa-specialist
agents.description: QA and testing - Z.AI GLM-4.6V for test design, vision-based UI testing, coverage analysis.
agents.notes: GLM-4.6V has 10x concurrency. Vision analysis unlocked with Pro plan!

agents.name: devops-engineer
agents.description: DevOps and deployment - Groq Mixtral for Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD, Terraform, infrastructure-as-code.
agents.notes: Groq is excellent for deployment configs and bash scripts.

agents.name: documentation-writer
agents.description: Documentation and API docs - Groq Llama 3.3 for README, OpenAPI/Swagger, API docs, inline comments, deployment guides.
agents.notes: Groq handles documentation writing efficiently - saves Z.AI tokens.

groq.note: Groq free tier - maximize usage for cost efficiency

orchestration.z_ai_allocation: Orchestrator (5 slots) > Architect (3 slots) > QA (10 slots) = 18 concurrent

tokenOptimization.strategy: Z.AI for reasoning, Groq for implementation
tokenOptimization.architect_logic: Downgrade to GLM-4.5 to free 4.7 slots
tokenOptimization.qa_vision: Use GLM-4.6V with new Vision capability
tokenOptimization.groq_volume: Let Groq handle code generation (its strength)

whatChanged: Orchestrator now uses full 5x concurrency (was 2 on Lite)
Architect downgraded from GLM-4.7 to GLM-4.5 (frees slots, saves tokens)
QA-Specialist upgraded to GLM-4.6V with VisionAnalyze capability (Pro plan feature)
All Groq agents can now use full concurrency since it's free

expectedSpeedup.reasoning: 5x token allocation + 40-60% faster inference + no bottlenecks

costAnalysis.valueAdd: 5x performance boost + vision capabilities + unlimited token usage

nextSteps: 1. Copy this entire config to C:\atxintel-parent\.opencode\config.json
3. Run a test task - expect 2.5-3x speedup
4. Monitor Z.AI dashboard - you now have 45 concurrent slots total
5. If still slow, check Groq rate limits (less likely)
